skill.name: stitch-ui-design
skill.description: Expert guide for creating effective prompts for Google Stitch AI UI design tool. Use when user wants to design UI/UX in Stitch, create app interfaces, generate mobile/web designs, or needs help crafting prompts.
skill.summary: Expert guide for crafting effective prompts in Google Stitch AI UI design tool
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown files with guidance for Google Stitch AI UI design tool. All static analyzer findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code formatting, not shell execution; cryptographic algorithm flags are documentation text misinterpreted by scanner. No executable code, network requests, or dangerous patterns present.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick characters in markdown files as shell command execution. These are actually markdown code fences used to format example prompts, not executable code. Documentation skills contain no shell commands.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backtick occurrences are within markdown code blocks (```) used to display example prompts. No Ruby, shell, or any executable code exists in this documentation-only skill.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Scanner misinterpreted documentation text containing words like 'crypto', 'encrypt', or component names as actual cryptographic code. This is a documentation file with no cryptographic implementation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md and reference files contain example prompts for UI design - no cryptographic algorithms or encryption code exists.

high_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
high_findings.description: Scanner misinterpreted benign documentation words as reconnaissance patterns. No network scanning or system probing code exists.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged words appear in legitimate documentation context describing UI design concepts, not security scanning functionality.

content.user_title: Create UI Designs with Google Stitch
content.value_statement: This skill helps users craft effective prompts for Google Stitch AI UI design tool. It provides best practices, example prompts, and advanced techniques to generate professional web and mobile interfaces.
actual_capabilities: Guide users in crafting effective prompts for Google Stitch AI UI generator
Provide best practices for specifying UI components, layouts, and visual styles
Offer example prompts for various design scenarios (dashboards, e-commerce, mobile apps)
Explain how to create multi-screen app flows with iterative refinement
Show how to specify responsive behavior and platform-specific designs
Teach users how to export designs to HTML/CSS, Figma, and code
limitations: Skill only provides prompting guidance - it does not generate designs directly
Does not have access to Google Stitch API or tool execution capabilities
Cannot verify or test generated designs - only offers prompting advice
Google Stitch is an experimental tool with evolving capabilities

use_cases.title: Learn Stitch Prompting Basics
use_cases.description: New users wanting to understand how to write effective prompts for Google Stitch to generate their first UI designs
use_cases.target_user: Design beginners, developers trying AI design tools

use_cases.title: Improve Design Output Quality
use_cases.description: Users who have tried Stitch but get generic results and want to learn specific techniques to get better, more tailored designs

use_cases.title: Create Multi-Screen App Flows
use_cases.description: Users building complete applications who need guidance on prompting for multiple connected screens and responsive layouts

prompt_templates.title: Basic Single Screen Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [component type] with [specific features], using [color scheme] theme and [layout style] design.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple page or component design request

prompt_templates.title: E-commerce Product Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an e-commerce product page with hero image gallery, add-to-cart button, reviews section, and related products carousel. Use clean minimalist design with [accent color] accents and generous white space.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a complete product page with multiple sections

prompt_templates.title: Mobile App Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a [screen name] screen for a [app type] mobile app. Include [list of features and components]. Use [design style] with [color palette]. Add [interactive states] and [navigation elements].
prompt_templates.scenario: Mobile app screen with specific UI components

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Screen App Flow
prompt_templates.prompt:
Create a [app type] app with:
- [screen 1] with [features]
- [screen 2] with [features]
- [screen 3] with [features]
Use [design system] style and ensure responsive behavior across mobile and desktop.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete multi-screen application with multiple pages

output_examples.input: How do I create a dashboard with Stitch?
output_examples.output:
To create a dashboard with Google Stitch, follow these principles:

1. Be Specific: Instead of 'Create a dashboard', use 'Member dashboard with course modules grid, progress tracking bar, and community feed sidebar using purple theme and card-based layout'

2. Define Visual Style: Include color schemes, design aesthetics

3. List Components: Specify each UI element needed

Example prompt: 'Analytics dashboard with KPI cards, line chart for trends, date range picker, and export button. Use modern dark theme with accent colors for data visualization.'

output_examples.input: What should I include for mobile app prompts?
output_examples.output:
For mobile apps, specify:

- Platform: iOS, Android, or cross-platform
- Screen dimensions and responsive behavior
- Navigation pattern (tab bar, drawer, stack)
- Native components for the platform
- Touch-friendly sizing and spacing

Example: 'Mobile app login screen (iOS style) with email/password fields, social auth buttons (Google, Apple), and forgot password link. Use iOS human interface guidelines with SF Pro font.'

best_practices: Always specify visual details: colors, typography, spacing, and design style to avoid generic AI outputs
List each screen explicitly for multi-screen apps - Stitch will confirm before generating multiple designs
Include functional requirements: button actions, form validation, navigation patterns, and user flows
anti_patterns: Using vague prompts like 'Create a nice login page' - results in generic, unusable designs
Forgetting to specify platform (mobile vs desktop) leading to wrong layout expectations
Not defining visual style, causing AI to output random or inconsistent aesthetics

faq.question: What is Google Stitch?
faq.answer: Google Stitch is an experimental AI UI generator powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash that transforms text prompts and visual references into functional UI designs for web and mobile applications.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Google Stitch?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides prompting guidance and best practices. It does not have access to execute Google Stitch or generate designs directly.

faq.question: What file formats can Stitch export to?
faq.answer: Google Stitch supports export to HTML/CSS, Figma, and code formats depending on the design created.

faq.question: Can I create responsive designs with Stitch?
faq.answer: Yes, specify responsive behavior in your prompts. Describe how the design should adapt from mobile (320px) to desktop (1440px) dimensions.

faq.question: How do I get better results from Stitch?
faq.answer: Be extremely specific in your prompts: list all components, specify colors and themes, describe layouts in detail, and include functional requirements like button actions and navigation.

faq.question: Is Google Stitch free to use?
faq.answer: Google Stitch is currently an experimental tool available through Google Labs. Usage terms may vary; check Google Labs for current availability and pricing.
